question.text: What was the penalty imposed on HSBC as DDP in the Pathway Finance case?

answer.text: Rs. 25 lakhs and a warning letter
Rs. 5 lakhs under Section 15A(b) of the SEBI Act
Rs. 1 crore and suspension of DDP license
Rs. 16 lakhs under Section 15A(b) of the SEBI Act

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The order imposed a penalty of Rs. 5 lakhs upon HSBC DDP under Section 15A(b) of the SEBI Act for violation of clause 5.1.4 of Operational guidelines. **The Trap:** Rs. 16 lakhs was the penalty in the SHCIL case (Case 11.2), not the HSBC case. **Cross-Exam Context:** SEBI penalty provisions under various sections are frequently tested in NISM Series III-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Keep the two case study penalties distinct: HSBC = Rs. 5 lakhs (reporting delay), SHCIL = Rs. 16 lakhs (multiple violations).
